data.title: India's HEC Infra Projects hits exchange-allowed upper limit on winning work order
data.description:
** Shares of HEC Infra Projects up 10%, the upper limit of exchange-allowed trading band

** Construction firm gets work order worth 96.2 mln rupees ($1.1 mln) for engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) services from Blue Pine Energy

** Stock up ~13.5% so far in April, after falling for three straight months

** More than 12,000 shares traded, 2.4x its 30-day moving avg

** HECF trims YTD losses to ~6%

($1 = 85.7020 Indian rupees)

data.title: Ericsson CEO: Tariffs Less on Manufacturing Side, More on Components Side
Ericsson CEO: Already Have a Geopolitically Resilient Supply Chain
Fenghua Advanced Tech to Divest Stake in Unit to Guangdong Electronics Information Industry
data.description:
Guangdong Fenghua Advanced Technology plans to divest its 21.5% holdings in Guangdong Fenghua New Energy to Guangdong Electronics Information Industry Group, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on April 12.

Guangdong Electronics Information Industry will buy the stake after being the only bidder on the Guangdong United Assets and Equity Exchange Center.

The stake had an initial bid price of 84.6 million yuan, the Chinese electronic equipment manufacturer said.

The company's shares rose over 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Market Chatter: GAC to Launch L3 Self-Driving Car in Q4
data.description:
Guangzhou Automobile Group , or GAC, will debut its first Level 3 autonomous vehicle in the fourth quarter, Yicai Global reported Monday.

The car manufacturer plans to deliver cars pre-installed with Level 4 autonomous driving technology in 2025, aiming for large-scale operations in 2026 and full release by 2027, according to the report.

GAC's joint venture with Didi Autonomous Driving, Andi Technology, will begin mass production of L4 vehicles in 2025, targeting rollout in parts of Guangzhou and Beijing in 2026, the report said.

Shares of the company were down 1% in recent trade.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Turkish central bank 2024 loss around $18 billion
data.description:
Turkey's central bank posted a loss of 700.4 billion lira ($18.4 billion) in 2024, according to its balance sheet published in the Official Gazette on Tuesday.

In 2023, the bank had posted a loss of 818.2 billion lira (or $25 billion at that time). The loss, stemming from a foreign exchange-protected deposit scheme, prompted the central bank to pass on distributing profit to the Treasury in 2023 and now in 2024.

The central bank will convene its general assembly on April 30 in Ankara to discuss the 2024 results.

($1 = 38.0259 liras)

data.title: Saudi Arabia's Edaa Initiates Securities Quantity Increase for Raoom Trading
data.description:
Saudi Arabia's Securities Depository Center, or Edaa, implemented a securities quantity increase for Raoom Trading (SASE:4144).

The increase will be reflected on the accounts of the Saudi Arabian glass manufacturing and trading company's eligible holders on Tuesday, according to a same-day release.

data.title: Asian Auto Stocks Rise on Hopes for Potential Tariff Exemptions
data.description:
By Kosaku Narioka

Asian auto stocks rose as President Trump's comments on potential tariff exemptions for carmakers further lessened fears about escalating trading frictions between the U.S. and its trading partners.

Toyota Motor shares were recently 4.9% higher on Tuesday in Tokyo, Honda Motor was up 3.9% and Nissan Motor was 3.0% higher. In Seoul, Hyundai Motor was up 4.6%, while Jaguar Land Rover-owner Tata Motors was 4.7% higher in Mumbai.

President Trump said Monday that he was considering some short-term tariff exemptions to help car companies that are looking to switch making parts from Canada and Mexico to the U.S.

"They need a little bit of time because they're going to make them here," he said about the parts, without giving additional details.

The comments came after the U.S. administration late Friday announced tariff exemptions on smartphones, laptops and several other electronics products, leading to a relief rally in Asian markets on Monday.

Trump said that he exempted smartphones and certain electronics from recent tariffs in order to help U.S. companies, including Apple.

The Trump administration's 25% tariffs on foreign-made automobiles took effect on April 3. The U.S. government said components that are compliant with the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement would remain tariff-free until it develops a system to measure their U.S. content.

Automakers are scrambling to respond to the tariffs, which could hurt sales or damage profitability in the U.S. market.

Earlier this month, Nissan decided to continue two shifts for producing the Rogue midsize crossover SUV at its Tennessee plant, scrapping an earlier plan to scale back to one shift in order to maintain localized production volume in the U.S.

In March, Hyundai said it would invest an additional $21 billion in U.S.-based car manufacturing and supply chains for critical materials. Trump credited the South Korean company's investment to his aggressive use of tariffs.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: A Chinese producer offers Polypropylene (PP Homopolymer) in Vietnam
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Vietnam reported that a producer from China is heard to have announced its offer for PP Homopolymer grades at the 945-955/mt levels.These offers are for May 2025 shipment on CFR Ho Chi Minh port basis.

data.title: A producer based in China offers Polypropylene (PP Homopolymer) in Vietnam
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Vietnam stated that a Chinese producer is heard to have offered its PP Homopolymer grades at the 925-935/mt levels.These offers are for May 2025 shipment on CFR Ho Chi Minh port basis.

data.title: AnteoTech Appoints Interim CEO
data.description:
AnteoTech appointed Merrill Gray as interim Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director, effective April 16, according to a Tuesday filing with the Australian bourse.

Gray will succeed David Radord who is stepping down on Tuesday but will continue to serve as strategic advisor for the next four weeks, the filing said.

data.title: KT Corp To Divest 5 Billion Won Worth Of Own Shares
data.description:
KT Corp :

  • KT CORP: TO DIVEST 5 BILLION WON WORTH OF OWN SHARES

data.title: Publicis Groupe Q1 Revenue Up; Outlook Confirmed
data.description:
Publicis Groupe said Tuesday that revenue increased year over year in the first quarter of 2025 ended March 31.

The French public relations group's revenue was 3.54 billion euros, compared with 3.23 billion euros previously.

Looking ahead, the company confirmed its forecast of growth between 4% and 5% for 2025, while it expects its second-quarter results to grow in the 4% and 5% range.

data.title: U.S. Treasurys Seem to Tentatively Gain GroundMarket Talk
data.description: It was "something of a relief" to see Treasurys gaining ground Monday, says Pepperstone's Michael Brown in a note. This was likely a result of the real money selling from last week, as capital fled from the U.S., abating a touch, the senior research strategist says. Both the 10-year and 30-year Treasury yields fell Monday, and both are now trading "a fair way off the 'panicky' levels seen last week," he says. Still, Treasurys seem to be on a relatively shaky ground, particularly with there being no sign of policy incoherence abating any time soon and levels of uncertainty remaining extremely elevated, he says. The 10-year Treasury yield is down 2 basis points to 4.341%; the 30-year yield is off 3 basis points to 4.769%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Biotechnology Company Galapagos' CFO/COO Steps Down
data.description:
Galapagos said Tuesday Thad Huston is stepping down as chief financial officer and chief operating officer, with effect from Aug. 1.

The biotechnology company said a successor is expected to be named in the coming months.

data.title: Ericsson CEO: Think There Will Be a Minor Impact From Tariffs, Should Be Manageable
